description: Slice1/Caps: add a shared deployment-profile module exporting a DeploymentCapabilities type { portalDomain: { provisioner: 'temporal' | 'direct' }, trustForwardedHost: boolean }.
Slice1/Caps: resolveDeploymentCapabilities() reads DEPLOYMENT_PROFILE and returns the appliance capabilities for 'appliance'.
Slice1/Caps: resolveDeploymentCapabilities() defaults to the hosted capabilities (provisioner 'temporal', trustForwardedHost false) for unset/unknown DEPLOYMENT_PROFILE.
Slice1/Provisioner: define the PortalDomainProvisioner interface (register/refresh/retry/disable) and ProvisionContext { knex, tenant, domain?, existing, canonicalHost }.

Slice1/Provisioner: extract today's cloud behavior into temporalProvisioner (upsert pending_dns + enqueuePortalDomainWorkflow + DNS/cert status messages + disable cleanup workflow), behavior-preserving.
Slice1/Provisioner: directProvisioner.register upserts the portal_domains row with status 'active' and a proxy-contract status message, enqueuing no workflow.
Slice1/Provisioner: directProvisioner.disable deletes the portal_domains row (relying on OTT onDelete CASCADE).
Slice1/Provisioner: directProvisioner.refresh and .retry are idempotent ensure-active no-ops (no transient/failed states).
Slice1/Provisioner: directProvisioner rejects a domain equal to the NEXTAUTH_URL host (in addition to the existing canonicalHost rejection) with a clear error.
Slice1/Provisioner: directProvisioner.register populates verificationDetails with proxy fields { proxy_target_host, proxy_target_port: 3000, forward_host_header: true }.
Slice1/Provisioner: provisioner factory selects temporal vs direct driver from caps.portalDomain.provisioner.
Slice1/Actions: requestPortalDomainRegistrationAction delegates its side effect to provisioner.register (validation + auth + response-shaping stay in the action).
Slice1/Actions: refresh/retry/disable actions delegate to the corresponding provisioner methods; cloud status-message literals move into temporalProvisioner.
Slice1/Types: PortalDomainStatusResponse gains a mode: 'temporal' | 'direct' field, set by the action from the resolved provisioner.
Slice1/UI: ClientPortalDomainSettings renders the BYO-proxy contract help text + checklist (not CNAME-to-canonical) when mode === 'direct'.
Slice1/UI: the direct-mode checklist links to the operator proxy setup doc.
Slice1/UI: i18n strings for msp/settings added for the proxy-contract help text and checklist.
Slice1/Middleware: resolveRequestHost() returns the Host hostname, falling back to X-Forwarded-Host only when caps.trustForwardedHost is set; middleware vanity detection uses it.
Slice1/Config: helm/templates/deployment.yaml injects DEPLOYMENT_PROFILE from .Values.deploymentProfile | default 'hosted' into alga-core.
Slice1/Config: appliance alga-core.single-node.yaml sets deploymentProfile: appliance.
Slice1/Config: appliance temporal-worker.single-node.yaml drops portal-domain-workflows from taskQueue and removes the empty portalDomain block.
Slice1/Docs: operator setup + troubleshooting doc (nginx + caddy snippets preserving Host and X-Forwarded-Proto, DNS/TLS expectations, Host-not-forwarded failure).
Slice2/Diagnostics: provisioner maintains a Redis set of active vanity hostnames — register adds, disable removes.
Slice2/Diagnostics: a request arriving on an active vanity Host records a last-seen timestamp in Redis (placement resolved per the edge/node-runtime open question).
Slice2/Diagnostics: getPortalDomainStatusAction surfaces an 'active but never seen on its Host' signal (active beyond a threshold with no last-seen).
Slice2/Diagnostics: Settings card renders the ⚠ 'no requests have reached <domain> yet — check your proxy forwards the Host header' when that signal is set.
Slice2/Diagnostics: middleware emits a rate-limited log-warn when X-Forwarded-Host names an active domain but Host does not (Host-rewrite tell-tale).
